verificationStatus: not-verified-against-gazette — this risk matrix is a constructed representation based on standard risk management practices adapted for Act 854 NCII requirements; it has not been verified against any officially published NACSA instrument
title: NCII Cyber Security Risk Matrix (5x5)
description: Standard 5x5 likelihood-impact risk matrix for NCII cyber security risk assessment under Act 854. Aligned with ISO 31000:2018 and the NACSA Directive on Risk Assessment Methodology.

bands.actionRequired: Accept and monitor. Record in risk register with annual review. No escalation required. Manage within existing operational procedures.
bands.nacsaObligation: No specific NACSA reporting obligation. Include in annual risk assessment submission.

bands.actionRequired: Treat within defined timeframe. Develop treatment plan with assigned owner and target date. Monitor monthly. Escalate to CISO/NCII Compliance Officer.
bands.nacsaObligation: Include in risk assessment report to CE and sector lead. Treatment plan required.
bands.responseTimeframe: Treatment plan within 90 days; implementation within 6 months

bands.actionRequired: Treat as priority. Immediate treatment plan with executive ownership. Report to CE and sector lead within 14 days. Weekly monitoring until residual risk is reduced. Board reporting required.
bands.nacsaObligation: Fast-track reporting to CE and sector lead within 14 days. Risk acceptance requires board-level approval and NACSA notification within 30 days.
bands.responseTimeframe: Treatment plan within 30 days; critical controls within 60 days

bands.actionRequired: Immediate action required. Emergency treatment plan with CEO/board ownership. Immediate reporting to CE and sector lead. Daily monitoring. Consider temporary service restrictions or compensating controls.
bands.nacsaObligation: Immediate reporting to CE and sector lead. Risk acceptance at Critical level requires explicit NACSA acknowledgement. CE may exercise s28 powers to direct remediation. Non-remediation may trigger enforcement under s50-51.
bands.responseTimeframe: Emergency response within 48 hours; treatment plan within 14 days
